step.title: Generate Auth flow goals (phase=auth)
step.rule: Fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) and error handling goal (phase=error-handling) MUST exist in goals.yaml before this step runs. If either is missing, ABORT with error.
Auth flows are counted in N_auth_flows ONLY — never in per-BC N_actions (no double counting).
One goal per auth flow — never batch multiple flows into one goal.
All inputs read from docs/architecture/* files on disk (G-16), never from conversation context.

substep.title: Read auth flow inventory from discovery output
substep.action: Read docs/architecture/cross-cutting.md. Extract the auth flows section written by discover.xml Step 6.2.

Build AUTH_FLOWS list — each entry has: flow_name (Register, Login, Logout, Token Refresh, Password Reset), description, steps involved, and the flow's bc_name (the identity/auth BC the action lives in).
substep.rule: Only include flows confirmed during discovery. If Password Reset was marked "not in scope", exclude it (AU-04 is conditional).
Token Refresh is a valid auth flow if discovered — do not skip it.

substep.title: Resolve dependency goal IDs
substep.action: Read goals.yaml from disk. Find the goal with phase=fixtures — store its ID as FIXTURES_GOAL_ID.
Find the goal with phase=error-handling — store its ID as ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID.
Find the goal with phase=auth-bootstrap — store its ID as AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID (created by step 3.16a; always present when N_auth_flows >= 1, which is true here since auth flows exist).

check.action: ABORT with error: "Cannot generate auth goals: no auth-bootstrap goal (phase=auth-bootstrap) found in goals.yaml — step 3.16a must run before 3.19 when auth flows exist"
ABORT with error: "Cannot generate auth goals: no fixtures goal (phase=fixtures) found in goals.yaml — ensure fixtures step ran first"
ABORT with error: "Cannot generate auth goals: no error handling goal (phase=error-handling) found in goals.yaml — ensure error handling step ran first"

substep.title: Generate one goal skeleton per auth flow via goal-create

per-flow.action: Set AUTH_AREA = the directory of RESOLVE_CONTROLLER_PATH({bc_name}, {AuthAction}) (3.17.0, the single source of truth for controller paths) — the coarse controller directory contexts/{bc_name}/app/src/Http/Controller/ (or the projects/{app}/src/{Module}/ deployable-app dir when the discovery override applies). COARSE deliverable directory (the elaborator's tree-read anchor; it refines into the precise controller/request/response/route/E2E footprint at dispatch against real files). NOT a per-file path, NOT a glob.
Call the goal-create MCP tool with a roadmap SKELETON:

params.param: Short title, <=120 chars. Pattern: "Auth: implement {flow_name} endpoint with {key_behavior}" Examples: - "Auth: implement user registration with password hashing and session token" - "Auth: implement login endpoint with credential validation and JWT response" - "Auth: implement logout endpoint with session/token invalidation" - "Auth: implement token refresh endpoint with session renewal" - "Auth: implement password reset flow with email verification"

per-flow.action: After each successful goal-create, record the returned goal ID. Append to AUTH_GOAL_IDS list.

check.action: Print error with full context and ABORT — do not continue to next flow.

per-flow.rule: Every auth goal references the existing security config + User entity from the auth-bootstrap dependency (AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID, step 3.16a) — no per-flow goal creates security.yaml or the User entity (D3 determinism gate). The elaborator confirms this against the real tree.
Never hard-code test credentials in goal descriptions — always reference docs/architecture/test-environment.md (GM-05, AU-10).
description field max 120 chars (MaxGoalDescriptionLength). Detailed AU/PC criteria are authored by /tmux:elaborate, not inlined into the skeleton.

NO acceptance/validate/scope/context/investigation_config/not_in_scope params — roadmap skeleton; /tmux:elaborate authors AU-01..AU-10 + PC-01..PC-17 (and the Playwright-vs-API-only deliverable/validate set) at dispatch against the REAL tree (design §5/§6). NEVER run `tmux-cli rules match` here.

substep.title: Record auth goal IDs for downstream dependencies
substep.action: Store AUTH_GOAL_IDS list as a session variable for use by subsequent steps (controller action goals).

Print summary: "Auth skeletons created: {{N_auth_flows}} goals (phase=auth, status=roadmap, depends_on=[{{FIXTURES_GOAL_ID}}, {{ERROR_HANDLING_GOAL_ID}}, {{AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID}}]), IDs: [{{AUTH_GOAL_IDS}}]"
substep.rule: The real auth-infrastructure prerequisite for protected endpoints is AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID (security firewall/provider + User entity), NOT "all auth goals". Subsequent auth_required controller action goals (3.18.2) MUST depend on AUTH_BOOTSTRAP_GOAL_ID; the legacy "all AUTH_GOAL_IDS" dependency is retained for 401/403 flow E2E assertions but is no longer the source of the security infra itself.
